title: Stomp Effect
result_description: Each stomp shoves the centered video down the frame, revealing hook text underneath one line at a time.

inputs.name: main_clip
inputs.what_to_film: Walk in and stomp several times then jump, looking up to lead the eye toward the reveal. Filmed widescreen (phone sideways on tripod).

capture_requirements.note: shot widescreen on purpose so it sits centered in a 9:16 frame

edit_steps.note: set the canvas to vertical 9:16; the widescreen clip sits centered with empty space below
zero out position so the clip snaps to center
trim the front and tail so it cuts straight into the action
press M on each stomp frame (audio spikes confirm timing) to mark all stomps
two frames before each stomp set a locking keyframe, then on the marker drag the clip down
small stomps nudge ~60px each (-60, -120, -180); lock-before-then-move so it only jumps on the stomp
big jumps move much more (even numbers like -600, -1200) to clear room for the text
add the hook text in stacked lines (e.g. black on white) positioned so each reveal exposes one line
option+drag a copy of the keyframed clip ABOVE the text, exact same timing, so the video covers the text until each stomp slides it away
optional CTA line (e.g. read the caption) with an arrow emoji pasted in

result.description: Stomps drive the centered video downward in keyframed steps, progressively uncovering the hook text.
success_criteria: Video only moves on each stomp (locked between keyframes)
Each step reveals one line of text
Top copy keeps the text hidden until its stomp
